perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall, upon termination of this Agreement, or upon written request of Discloser, whichever is earlier, immediately, but not later than 10 days after any notice thereof by Discloser, return (or destroy at Discloser’s option) all copies of such Discloser’s Confidential Information and certify in writing its compliance with this requirement, except that Recipient may retain a copy of such Confidential Information solely for archival purpose .
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall, upon termination of this Agreement, or upon written request of Discloser, whichever is earlier, immediately, but not later than 10 days after any notice thereof by Discloser, return (or destroy at Discloser’s option) all copies of such Discloser’s Confidential Data and certify in writing its compliance with this requirement, except that Recipient may retain a copy of such Confidential Information solely for archival purpose .
perturbation.explanation: Here, we change 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data', but in the same sentence, use 'Confidential Information' for archival purpose. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether the exception for archival purposes applies to 'Confidential Data' or only to 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership and Return of Confidential Information . Confidential Information disclosed by Discloser shall remain the property of such Discloser, and no license or other rights to such Discloser ’s Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby. Recipient shall reproduce the symbols, legends or other p roprietary notices affixed to Confidential Information, and shall not, nor permit any third party to, remove, add or modify the same . Recipient shall, upon termination of this Agreement, or upon written request of Discloser, whichever is earlier, immediately, but not later than 10 days after any notice thereof by Discloser, return (or destroy at Discloser’s option) all copies of such Discloser’s Confidential Data and certify in writing its compliance with this requirement, except that Recipient may retain a copy of such Confidential Information solely for archival purpose .
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data' in the first part of the sentence but retains 'Confidential Information' later in the same sentence for archival purposes. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text also includes the modified sentence, creating a conflicting instruction regarding whether the archival exception applies to 'Confidential Data' or only 'Confidential Information'. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the parties, as Recipient , hereby promise s and agree s to receive and hold Confidential Information in confidence, and to protect and safeguard Confidential Information against unauthorized use or disclosure using at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords to its own confidential information of lik e importance, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the parties, as Receiver , hereby promise s and agree s to receive and hold Confidential Information in confidence, and to protect and safeguard Confidential Information against unauthorized use or disclosure using at least the same degree of care as Receiver accords to its own confidential information of lik e importance, but in no case less than ordinary care.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Recipient' to 'Receiver' and 'reasonable care' to 'ordinary care'. It's not explicitly stated if the obligations of the “Receiver” are the same as the “Recipient”. Furthermore, the term 'ordinary care' is far more lenient that reasonable care, which can create a legal dispute when determining who is liable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations . Each of the parties, as Receiver , hereby promise s and agree s to receive and hold Confidential Information in confidence, and to protect and safeguard Confidential Information against unauthorized use or disclosure using at least the same degree of care as Receiver accords to its own confidential information of lik e importance, but in no case less than ordinary care. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, each party, as Recipient , further promise s and agree s: (a) not to, directly or indirectly, in any way, disclose, make accessible, reveal, report, pub lish, disseminate or transfer any Confidential Inf ormation to any third party; (b) not to use any Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, except in furtherance of the Purpose in acc ordance with this Agreement; and (c) to restrict access to Confi dential Information to those of its officers, directors and employees who have a legitimate need - to-know to carry out the Purpose and who are obligated to protect such Confidential Information pursuant to terms and conditions no less protective of Disclose r than those contained in this Agreement ; and (d) not to reproduce or copy Confidential Information except to the extent necessary to further the Purpose . Confidential Furthermore, the existence of any business negotiati ons, discussions or agreements in progress between the parties shall be kept confidential and shall not be disclosed without written approval of all the parties . For the purposes of this Agreement, Company shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other division, department, group, or unit other than Samsung KNOX Business and Technology Division .
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'Recipient' to 'Receiver' and 'reasonable care' to 'ordinary care.' The contradiction arises in the contradicted text because it uses both 'Receiver' and 'Recipient' and expects both to have the same obligation. The term 'ordinary care' is also far more lenient than reasonable care, which can create a legal dispute when determining who is liable. This inconsistency creates ambiguity and a contradiction regarding the standard of care expected of the parties, aligning with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition. The contradicted text expects all parties to adhere to the same standard.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by each of the parties to this Agreement. The English language text of this Agreement shall prevail over any translations thereof. If a contract is entered into by the parties in respect of the Purpose, this Agreement shall be deemed incorporated into such contract in addition to any confidential ity obligations set forth in such contract, if any , and the term of this Agreement is deemed to be extended (but not shorted) to be the same as that of the contract .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the issue discussed here and may not be changed or altered except in a memo signed by each of the parties to this Agreement. The English language text of this Agreement shall control over any translations thereof. If an agreement is entered into by the parties in respect of the Objective, this Agreement shall be deemed part of that agreement in addition to any secrecy duties set forth in that agreement, if any , and the duration of this Agreement is deemed to be extended (but not shorted) to be the same as that of the agreement .
perturbation.explanation: Replaced multiple terms with alternatives: 'entire agreement' -> 'complete understanding', 'subject matter' -> 'issue discussed', 'amended or modified' -> 'changed or altered', 'writing' -> 'memo', 'prevail' -> 'control', 'Purpose' -> 'Objective', 'contract' -> 'agreement', 'confidentiality obligations' -> 'secrecy duties', 'term' -> 'duration'. If those terms are equivalent is not stated, then creating in-text contradiction as those change may imply that it's a different definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement . This Agreement constitutes the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the issue discussed here and may not be changed or altered except in a memo signed by each of the parties to this Agreement. The English language text of this Agreement shall control over any translations thereof. If an agreement is entered into by the parties in respect of the Objective, this Agreement shall be deemed part of that agreement in addition to any secrecy duties set forth in that agreement, if any , and the duration of this Agreement is deemed to be extended (but not shorted) to be the same as that of the agreement .
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation details that multiple terms were replaced with alternatives, such as 'entire agreement' being replaced with 'complete understanding', and 'subject matter' with 'issue discussed'. The explanation states that if these terms are not equivalent, it creates an in-text contradiction, as the change may imply a different definition. The contradicted text uses the changed terms, indicating that the contract now uses those terms interchangeably without defining them. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', leading to conflicting obligations and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information is provided “AS IS” and “AS A VAILABLE” without any warranty, express, implied or otherwise, regarding such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Proprietary Data is provided “AS IS” and “AS A VAILABLE” without any guarantee, express, implied or otherwise, regarding such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data', and 'warranty' to 'guarantee' in only one instance. Makes it unclear whether the disclaimer applies to 'Proprietary Data'. Also, it refers back to 'Confidential Information' which then is an inconsistency.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Warranty . Proprietary Data is provided “AS IS” and “AS A VAILABLE” without any guarantee, express, implied or otherwise, regarding such Confidential Information. Nothing herein shall be construed as a commitment by any party to disclose any Confidential Inform ation, to commence or continue negotiations or to enter into a ny contract or business relationship. Neither this Agreement, nor the disclosure or receipt of Confidential Information, shall constitute or imply any promise or intention by any of the parties or their affiliates to develop, make, purchase or sell any present or future products or services. Any commitment to do or promise any of the foregoing must be in a separate writing signed by an authorized representative of each party. If any such agreemen t contains warranty provisions, those provisions shall prevail over the corresponding provisions in this Agreement. Each party shall bear its own fees, costs and expenses incurred in carrying out, or otherwise in relation to, this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' and the contradicted text also refers to 'Confidential Information'. The changed text alters 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' in one instance while still referencing 'Confidential Information'. This creates an inconsistency, making it unclear whether the disclaimer applies to both 'Proprietary Data' and 'Confidential Information', thereby creating a contradiction within the document. The explanation clarifies that there's a term mismatch/misalignment, which is a type of in-text contradiction.
